criteria.label: Define training and inference and state what happens to θ in each phase.
criteria.example: Write: training updates θ using labeled (x,y) and loss; inference runs ŷ=f_θ(x) with θ frozen and no y.

criteria.label: Map fit and predict to the training and inference phases.
criteria.example: Given clf.fit(X,y) and clf.predict(X_new), label each call as training or inference and list required inputs.

criteria.label: List operations present in training but absent in inference.
criteria.example: Name three: loss L(ŷ,y), backward pass, optimizer step — and note inference uses forward pass only.

criteria.label: Implement a minimal train-then-predict workflow without refitting on test data.
criteria.example: Split (X,y), fit LogisticRegression on train only, predict on test, report accuracy — no second fit on test.

criteria.label: Explain why model.eval() and no_grad matter for PyTorch inference.
criteria.example: State what each disables (dropout training behavior, gradient tensors) and why θ must not change at serving.
